items.title: PCI-SIG reveals PCIe 8.0 0.5v spec — first draft includes 1 TB/s bandwidth and new connector technology
items.description: PCI-SIG announces availability of the first PCIe Gen8 draft, reassures 256 GT/s data transfer rate, maintains backwards compatibility, evaluates new connector technology.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The PCI-SIG, the organization that oversees development of PCIe and adjacent standards, on Wednesday announced the availability of the PCIe 8.0 draft specification version 0.5, a major milestone. The first draft of the specification sets the architectural requirements and is designed to enable PCI-SIG members to start prototyping and submit their final proposals. Version 0.5 of the specification maintains that the transfer rate supported by the new interconnection will reach 256 GT/s, enabling up to 1 TB/s bi-directional bandwidth via an x16 configuration.Version 0.5 of the PCIe standard is the first full draft of the spec that locks in key conceptual targets and mechanisms, and outlines all major aspects of the architecture, including electrical, logical, compliance, and software. This means that PCI-SIG maintains a target bit rate of 256 GT/s; PAM4 signaling with forward error correction (FEC) and Flit Mode encoding; bandwidth-improving protocol enhancements; backward compatibility; and new connector technology that is now being evaluated. Meanwhile, since version 0.5 is not the final draft and not all parts of the specification are frozen, some electrical parameters and protocol optimizations can be tuned further.The new release is a major milestone as this is when hardware designers — large companies like AMD, Intel, and Nvidia, as well as IP or PHY vendors — may start early prototyping and architecture work, albeit with contingency plans for possible changes. The most important point is that the specification is mature enough, and development work can begin.Article continues below You may like PCI Express roadmap: The path to 1TB/s with PCI 8.0, the challenges of integration, and beyond First PCIe 6.0 SSD enters mass production with 28GB/s speeds, 5.5 million IOPS, and liquid cooling Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies (Image credit: PCI-SIG)One of the intriguing parts of the announcement is that PCI-SIG continues to evaluate new connector technology, which essentially means the current copper physical layer is getting uncomfortably close to its limits.Loss budgets, crosstalk, and reflections have become serious constraints for PCIe 5.0 and 6.0, but with PCIe 8.0 and its 256 GT/s bit rate — a data transfer rate no copper-based standard has ever achieved — they are likely going to become a nightmare. At such speeds, the traditional edge connector and motherboard routing may not deliver acceptable signal integrity without excessive power (for equalization) or latency (for FEC). As a result, PCI-SIG might look to redesign PCIe slots with better materials and tighter tolerances, or to shorten electrical paths once again while increasing the number of redrivers per link. In any case, since PCI-SIG wants to maintain backward compatibility, do not expect any drastic changes on the connector level.With this draft available, the PCIe 8.0 standard continues to progress toward final ratification in 2028. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: PCI-SIG reveals PCIe 8.0 0.5v spec — first draft includes 1 TB/s bandwidth and new connector technology
items.descriptionZh:
PCI-SIG（负责制定PCIe及相关标准）于周三宣布，PCIe 8.0规范草案0.5版本正式可用。这是一个重要的里程碑，标志着标准开发进入了关键阶段。该草案首次锁定了架构的核心概念和目标机制，勾勒出电气、逻辑、合规性及软件等所有主要架构方面。这意味着，PCI-SIG维持了256 GT/s的目标传输速率，采用PAM4信令并配合前向纠错（FEC）和Flit模式编码，同时引入了提升带宽的协议增强、保持向后兼容性，并对新的连接器技术进行评估（目前仍在测试中）。值得注意的是，0.5版本并非最终草案，部分规范尚未冻结，因此一些电气参数和协议优化仍可进一步调整。

性能参数方面，PCIe 8.0将实现256 GT/s的单通道传输速率，通过x16配置可提供高达1 TB/s的双向带宽。这一速率远超当前任何铜基标准，使得信号完整性成为主要挑战。在PCIe 5.0和6.0中，损耗预算、串扰和反射已成为严重制约因素；到了PCIe 8.0的256 GT/s，这些问题可能变得更为棘手。传统的边缘连接器和主板走线在如此高的速度下，若不以过高的功耗（用于均衡）或延迟（用于FEC）为代价，将无法保证可接受的信号完整性。因此，PCI-SIG可能会采用更好材料和更严格公差重新设计PCIe插槽，或者再次缩短电气路径并增加每条链路上的信号重驱动器数量。不过，由于要保持向后兼容，连接器层面预计不会有剧烈变化。

技术核心创新点在于：256 GT/s的速率本身就是一个里程碑，没有铜基标准曾经达到过；PAM4信令配合前向纠错（FEC）和Flit模式编码是应对高速传输噪声的关键技术；协议层面的增强进一步优化了带宽利用率。新的连接器技术正在评估中，可能采用新材料或更短的路径来减少信号衰减。

这一版本草案的发布对硬件设计师（如AMD、英特尔、英伟达等大公司，以及IP或PHY供应商）具有重要意义，他们可以开始早期原型开发和架构工作，但需为可能的变更保留应急预案。关键在于，规范已经足够成熟，开发工作可以启动。

应用场景方面，PCIe 8.0将主要用于高性能计算、数据中心、AI加速器、高端图形处理以及需要极高带宽的互连场景。其最终目标是在2028年获得正式批准，届时将为下一代计算系统提供突破性的I/O带宽。对于用户而言，这意味着服务器、GPU、存储设备之间的数据吞吐能力将进入TB/s级别，显著加速AI训练、科学计算和大规模数据处理等任务。

items.title: Nvidia invests $300 million in Corning to build three new US-based optical fiber plants — AI infrastructure deal would boost fiber production capacity by over 50%
items.description: Nvidia invests $300 million in Corning to help partners that deploy its hardware in the U.S. to get enough of optical fiber, grant Nvidia control over a significant chunk of domestic fiber production.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter When you have a dominant position in an industry, you should think not only about the competitiveness of your product, but also about eliminating bottlenecks in the sector to ensure smooth deployment of your hardware. This is exactly what Nvidia is doing by investing $300 million in Corning, enabling it to build three additional manufacturing facilities in the U.S. to produce optical fiber used to build AI data centers. As an added bonus, Nvidia will assume control of a significant part of American fiber production.The long-term agreement will see the construction of three new facilities in North Carolina and Texas to produce optical fiber, which will be supplied to builders of hyperscale data centers that deploy Nvidia AI hardware. As a result, Corning will increase its U.S. optical connectivity manufacturing output by a factor of 10 and expand domestic fiber production capacity by over 50%. The plants are expected to employ 3,000 people.Modern AI workloads require hundreds of thousands of AI accelerators working in concert and moving data at unprecedented scale. The only way to meet bandwidth and latency requirements for such deployments is to use optical fiber and photonics hardware for connectivity. As a result, the role of optical networking for AI data centers is hard to overestimate, and the skyrocketing demand for optical fiber at scale is easy to predict.Article continues below You may like Nvidia invests $4 billion into photonics firms in a bid to bolster data center interconnect supply chains AMD, Broadcom, and Nvidia join hyperscalers to define optical scale-up interconnect of the future for AI clusters Tech titans team up to form optical interconnect alliance to solve the AI buildout's big data bottleneck Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrowCorning is known for its expertise in glass science and optical physics and as the world's largest maker of optical cables with a 10.4% market share. With those credentials, the company is a good partner to fulfill that demand, so Nvidia's decision to team up with it is not surprising. Meanwhile, the press release by the two companies clearly states that the new plants will supply the optical connectivity hyperscale data centers use to deploy Nvidia-accelerated computing at scale,' so instead of helping the whole industry to get optical fiber, the company only helps the part of the industry that relies on its own hardware."AI is driving the largest infrastructure buildout of our time — and a once-in-a-generation opportunity to reinvigorate American manufacturing and supply chains," said Jensen Huang, founder and CEO of Nvidia. "Together with Corning, we are inventing the future of computing with advanced optical technologies — building the foundation for AI infrastructure where intelligence moves at the speed of light while advancing the proud tradition of Made in America."By investing in Corning, Nvidia not only ensures its partners will have enough optical fiber, but also gains control over a significant chunk of U.S. optical fiber production capacity, which is a good way to ensure the company's dominance in the American AI sector going forward. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: 英伟达投资3亿美元与康宁合作建设三家美国光纤工厂——AI基础设施交易将使光纤产能提升超50%
items.descriptionZh:
英伟达（Nvidia）正以3亿美元投资美国玻璃巨头康宁（Corning），计划在北卡罗来纳州和得克萨斯州新建三座光纤制造工厂，专门生产用于构建AI数据中心的光纤。这笔长期合作协议将使康宁的美国光连接制造产出提升10倍，国内光纤产能扩大超过50%，预计新增3000个就业岗位。此举背后是AI基础设施对数据传输能力的极端需求：现代AI工作负载需要数十万个AI加速器协同工作，数据移动规模前所未有，唯有光纤和光子学硬件才能满足带宽和延迟要求。英伟达创始人兼CEO黄仁勋表示，AI正驱动着“我们这个时代最大的基础设施建设”，而与美国制造商合作是“振兴美国制造和供应链”的千载难逢机遇。

从技术原理看，传统的铜缆连接在超大规模AI集群中已无法满足海量数据并行传输的需求，光纤凭借低损耗、高带宽和抗电磁干扰的优势成为必然选择。英伟达的AI加速器（如H100、B200等）在训练和推理过程中，每秒钟需要在数千个GPU之间交换数十TB的数据，光子互联技术通过将电信号转换为光信号，能以接近光速传输，极大降低延迟并提升能效。康宁作为全球最大的光缆制造商（市场份额10.4%），在玻璃科学和光学物理领域拥有深厚积累，其特种光纤产品专为高密度、高可靠性的数据中心设计，这正是英伟达选择合作的原因。

在性能参数方面，新工厂建成后，康宁的美国光纤产能将增加50%以上，光连接制造产出增长10倍，这意味着英伟达及其合作伙伴（如超大规模数据中心运营商）将获得稳定且优先的光纤供应。据行业分析，AI数据中心的建设正在急剧消耗全球光纤产能，光互联已成为继散热、供电之后的新瓶颈。英伟达的这项投资不仅确保了供应链安全，更获得了对美国本土光纤产能的显著控制权，从而巩固其在AI领域的市场主导地位——新工厂将专门为部署英伟达加速计算平台的超大规模数据中心供货，而非面向整个行业。

技术影响和应用场景方面，这一合作直接服务于AI基础设施的大规模部署，包括云服务商、企业AI训练集群以及边缘数据中心。黄仁勋强调，英伟达与康宁正在“以先进光学技术发明计算的未来”，让智能以光速流动。从产业格局来看，英伟达通过纵向整合关键材料——从GPU芯片到互联光纤——进一步锁定了竞争壁垒。同时，此举也响应了美国本土制造业回流政策，将被视为“美国制造”的成功范例。随着AI集群规模从万卡级向十万卡级迈进，光纤互联的产能和质量将直接决定AI算力的实际可用性，英伟达的提前布局可能重塑整个AI基础设施供应链的生态。

items.title: Survey shows that nearly half of Americans don't want new data centers built near their homes — 47% oppose the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood
items.description: According to a new survey conducted by Ipsos at the end of last year, almost half of all queried Americans said they would oppose an AI data center being built near their community.
items.content: Share Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter According to a new survey conducted by Ipsos at the end of last year, almost half of all queried Americans said they would oppose an AI data center being built near their community. This level of opposition is higher than that given to the creation of multi-apartment buildings, new apartment complexes, or mixed-use developments.This survey appears to highlight the growing opposition to data center construction in America. Around half of all previously announced data center projects have been delayed or cancelled entirely. Often this was for financial or component supply issues — such as Chinese power transformer shortages — but growing opposition from local lawmakers and communities about their impact on water and air quality, and electricity prices, has also been a factor.As resentment toward AI data centers appears to be on the rise, this problem for hyperscalers may only worsen as larger swathes of the American public oppose the creation of new “AI factories.”Article continues below You may like Local political revolts threaten to derail US data center projects Virginia voter support for new data centers collapses from 69% in 2023 to 35% in new poll Small Missouri town ousts city council after it approved $6 billion AI data center Millennials are the least concernedIn the survey, 4,000 U.S. residents were polled about their feelings towards the construction of new AI data centers near their communities. It was designed to gauge their fears around local environmental and service disruption, as well as the broader case of AI displacing human workers and upending social institutions.In total, 47% of respondents said they opposed the construction of new AI data centers in their neighborhood, with just 38% saying they supported it. That support was spread differently throughout various age ranges, however.Of those questioned, 50% of Millennial age respondents said they either somewhat or strongly supported the creation of new AI data centers in their neighborhood or local area. This was closely followed by 48% of Gen Z respondents. There was a large drop off after that, with only 38% of Gen X saying they supported their creation. Baby boomers were the least enthusiastic, with just 22% claiming they felt the same.In what is perhaps an example of the current U.S. administration’s influence and its entanglement with top tech firms, 49% of surveyed Republicans claimed they would support new data center creation in their local area. This stood in stark contrast to just 36% of Democrats. A causal trend could also be drawn from the fact that Republican voting states and counties tend to be more rural, with less economic activity. Data center projects do require construction, and there is the potential for local job creation.When it came to homeowners and renters, surprisingly, it was the homeowners who were more likely to support it, with 39% versus 36% of renters claiming they either somewhat or strongly supported new data center development in their neighborhood.Turning distrust into actionAlthough less than half of respondents in this survey offered strong opposition to data centers, the opposition they actually throw up to the construction of these new facilities is growing and having a serious effect.City councils that back data center projects are being voted out, other city councils are putting moratoriums on data center construction, and instances of more extreme violence towards AI companies and their employees are becoming more common. What to read next Six AI data centers proposed for a small town of 7,000, equal to 51 Walmart Supercenters in 17 square mile area Analytics group signals possible delays at 40% of AI data center construction sites Stargate AI data centers for OpenAI reportedly delayed by squabbles between partners Although this latest survey does show that there is some support for data center creation, the fact that the opposition is in the majority suggests that any data center projects that haven’t already been delayed or postponed are likely to face increasingly terse pushback that could derail their eventual development entirely.Considering this survey was from November 2025, too, there has been further evidence of pushback against hyperscalers in recent months.Who benefits?This latest survey was commissioned by real estate agent services company, Redfin, which highlighted how it had heard from frustrated homeowners about data center construction in their local areas. Citing one realtor operating in Prince George’s County, MD, they said there were concerns from residents that county officials were trading long-term community quality of life for projects that don’t directly benefit the people who live there.That’s a core component of many people’s misgivings with AI in general. Executives aren’t increasing their returns because of it, and companies are finding it hasn’t boosted productivity much either. It’s also becoming ever more expensive to run. Although there are outliers, the companies appearing to benefit the most from AI are the companies developing it, although they aren’t making anything close to a profit, aside from the chipmaking industry itself.Even the hyperscalers like Oracle, which have received hundreds of billions of dollars worth of compute orders since the large-scale AI buildout began in 2025, are heavily reliant on AI developers like OpenAI paying their bills. Considering OpenAI specifically is struggling to make the kind of money that would allow it to make good on those orders, the list of beneficiaries of new data center developments could be small.
items.author: Jon Martindale
items.titleZh: 调查显示，近半数美国人不希望家附近新建数据中心——47%反对在社区建设新AI数据中心
items.descriptionZh:
根据益普索（Ipsos）2025年底进行的一项最新调查，近一半受访美国人表示反对在其社区附近建设AI数据中心。这一反对比例甚至高于对多户公寓楼、新住宅区或混合用途开发项目的抵制。调查显示，美国民众对数据中心建设的抵触情绪正在加剧——此前宣布的数据中心项目中，约有一半已被推迟或完全取消。原因通常是财务或组件供应问题（如中国的电力变压器短缺），但当地立法机构和社区对其水质、空气质量及电价影响的担忧也日益成为重要因素。

随着AI数据中心引发的反感情绪上升，这种针对“AI工厂”的反对声浪可能进一步加剧超大规模云计算运营商的困境。调查发现，不同年龄段的支持度差异显著：50%的千禧一代（Millennials）表示“有些”或“强烈支持”在其社区或所在地区新建AI数据中心，Z世代紧随其后（48%），而X世代（Gen X）的支持率骤降至38%，婴儿潮一代（Baby Boomers）最低，仅为22%。政治倾向方面，49%的共和党受访者支持本地新建数据中心，而民主党仅为36%。这可能与共和党选民所在地区更偏乡村、经济活动较少有关——数据中心项目确实能带来建设和就业机会。出乎意料的是，房主比租房者更支持数据中心建设（39%对36%）。

尽管反对者尚未过半，但实际抵制行动正在产生实质性影响。支持数据中心项目的市议会成员被投票罢免，其他市议会则暂停了数据中心建设审批，针对AI公司及其员工的极端暴力事件也愈发常见。此前有报道称，弗吉尼亚州选民对新建数据中心的支持率从2023年的69%暴跌至35%；密苏里州一个小镇在批准价值60亿美元的AI数据中心后，整个市议会被选民推翻。

调查由房地产服务公司Redfin委托进行。Redfin指出，他们已收到房主对本地数据中心建设的抱怨。一名马里兰州乔治王子县的房地产经纪人表示，居民担心县官员用长期社区生活质量换取并不直接造福当地居民的项目。这正是许多人对AI整体持怀疑态度的核心：高管并未从中获得更高回报，企业也未发现其显著提升生产力，而运营成本却越来越高。虽然存在例外，但AI领域的主要受益者似乎是开发AI的企业本身——除了芯片制造商外，这些企业甚至远未实现盈利。即便是Oracle这样的超大规模云服务商，自2025年大规模AI建设启动以来已收到数千亿美元的算力订单，其收入也高度依赖OpenAI等AI开发商的付款。考虑到OpenAI自身正艰难实现足以履约的营收，新建数据中心的实际受益者可能非常有限。该调查于2025年11月进行，但此后数月已出现更多针对超大规模运营商的抵制案例。

items.title: Live updates from Elon Musk and Sam Altman’s court battle over the future of OpenAI
items.description:
Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead. 
Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Now, on Wednesday, May 6th,  Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, is taking the stand, and the courtroom is seeing testimony from former OpenAI exec Mira Murati via video.
Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.

Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.
In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.
People to Know
Plaintiff
Elon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAI
Steven Molo — lead counsel for plaintiff
Jared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family office
Shivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with Musk
Defendant
Sam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAI 
William Savitt — lead counsel for defendant
Greg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounder 
Ilya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounder
Judge
Yvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judge
Here’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
Sarah Eddy, an attorney representing OpenAI, got sarcastic with Zilis.
			
Shivon Zilis brainstormed possible scenarios for AI.
			
Musk offered Sam Altman a board seat at Tesla…
			
Shivon’s emails aren’t great for Musk.
			
The big sticking point for Brockman and Sutskever was control.
			
Sam Altman loves exclamation marks.
			
Mira Murati tells the court that she couldn’t trust Sam Altman’s words
			
Zilis’ past emails mentioned in court proceedings include her referencing a potential “conversion to for-profit” for OpenAI.
			
This is getting interesting.
			
Zilis sent Altman a text message of support after his 2023 ouster.
			
Zilis said another concern she had about Altman related to OpenAI’s potential deal with Helion.
			
Also in the spirit of clarifications this morning…
			
Zilis said she had major concerns about OpenAI’s board not being notified in advance of ChatGPT’s release.
			
Zilis said that the fallout from Altman’s 2023 ouster changed her view of OpenAI’s Microsoft deal.
			
When asked how much Musk works per week, Zilis laughed.
			
Musk’s team has called Shivon Zilis.
			
Murati says problems with Altman persisted after he returned to the company.
			
“OpenAI was at catastrophic risk of falling apart” when Altman was fired, Murati says.
			
We are seeing video testimony from Mira Murati’s deposition.
			
We are clearing up “a few inaccuracies from yesterday.”
			
We are taking care of some matters before the jury comes in.
			
Microsoft and OpenAI’s definition of AGI was just revealed.
			
The jurors look as bored as I feel.
			
Brockman steps down. We are looking at the video deposition of Robert Wu.
			
Brockman is telling the truth about considering removing Musk from the board.
			
Every time Molo makes a summary of Brockman’s testimony, Brockman objects to it.

items.content: Sam Altman and Elon Musk are facing off in a high-stakes trial that could alter the future of OpenAI and its most well-known product, ChatGPT. In 2024, Musk filed a lawsuit accusing OpenAI of abandoning its founding mission of developing AI to benefit humanity and shifting focus to boosting profits instead.Elon Musk, his financial manager and Neuralink CEO, Jared Birchall, and OpenAI cofounder Greg Brockman have already testified before the jury. Now, on Wednesday, May 6th, Shivon Zilis, a former OpenAI board member who shares four children with Musk, is taking the stand, and the courtroom is seeing testimony from former OpenAI exec Mira Murati via video.Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella is scheduled to appear on Monday, with OpenAI cofounder and former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever lined up to testify after that.Musk was a cofounder of OpenAI and claims that Altman and Brockman tricked him into giving the company money, only to turn their backs on their original goal. However, OpenAI says that “This lawsuit has always been a baseless and jealous bid to derail a competitor” in a bid to boost Musk’s own SpaceX / xAI / X companies that have launched Grok as a competitor to ChatGPT.In his lawsuit, Musk is asking for the removal of Altman and Brockman, and for OpenAI to stop operating as a public benefit corporation. Musk has also demanded that OpenAI’s nonprofit receive up to $150 billion in damages he’s asking for if he wins the case.People to KnowPlaintiffElon Musk — plaintiff, OpenAI cofounder and now CEO of rival xAISteven Molo — lead counsel for plaintiffJared Birchall — manager of Musk’s family officeShivon Zilis — former OpenAI board member who shares multiple children with MuskDefendantSam Altman — defendant, CEO of OpenAIWilliam Savitt — lead counsel for defendantGreg Brockman — president of OpenAI as well as a cofounderIlya Sutskever — former chief scientist at OpenAI and a cofounderJudgeYvonne Gonzalez Rogers — aka YGR, trial judgeHere’s all the latest on the trial between Musk and Altman:
items.author: Elizabeth Lopatto
items.titleZh: 埃隆·马斯克与萨姆·奥尔特曼关于OpenAI未来的法庭之争的最新动态
items.descriptionZh:
在一起可能重塑人工智能行业格局的高风险诉讼中，OpenAI联合创始人兼首席执行官山姆·奥特曼与另一位联合创始人、现xAI首席执行官埃隆·马斯克对簿公堂。这场庭审的焦点在于OpenAI是否背离了其创始使命——开发造福全人类的通用人工智能（AGI），转而沦为以营利为核心的商业实体。马斯克在2024年提起的诉状中指控，奥特曼和格雷格·布罗克曼通过虚假承诺引导他为公司注资，随后却抛弃了最初的公益目标。

截至2026年5月6日，庭审已进入关键证人出庭阶段。马斯克、他的家族理财室管理人贾里德·伯查尔以及OpenAI联合创始人布罗克曼已先后作证。当天，与马斯克育有四个孩子的前OpenAI董事会成员希文·齐利斯站上了证人席，前OpenAI高管米拉·穆拉蒂则通过视频提供了证词。微软首席执行官萨提亚·纳德拉将于下周一出庭，而OpenAI联合创始人兼前首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克弗也将随后作证。

马斯克作为OpenAI的初始联合创始人，声称奥特曼和布罗克曼诱骗他提供了资金，导致他在非营利模式下投入了大量财力和声誉。然而，OpenAI方面回应称，“这起诉讼始终是一场毫无根据且出于嫉妒、意图打压竞争对手的闹剧”，旨在为马斯克旗下的xAI、SpaceX及X公司（已推出ChatGPT的竞品Grok）谋利。

在庭审中，马斯克要求法院罢免奥特曼和布罗克曼在OpenAI的职务，并强制该公司停止以“公益公司”形式运营，恢复其非营利本质。此外，如果胜诉，马斯克还要求OpenAI的非营利实体获得高达1500亿美元的赔偿金。

**本案关键人物：**
- **原告方**：埃隆·马斯克（OpenAI联合创始人，现xAI首席执行官）；首席律师史蒂文·莫洛；马斯克家族理财室管理人贾里德·伯查尔；前OpenAI董事会成员希文·齐利斯（与马斯克育有共同子女）。
- **被告方**：山姆·奥特曼（OpenAI首席执行官）；首席律师威廉·萨维特；OpenAI总裁兼联合创始人格雷格·布罗克曼；前OpenAI首席科学家伊利亚·苏茨克弗。
- **主审法官**：伊冯·冈萨雷斯·罗杰斯（YGR）。

**技术背景与核心争议：**
虽然本案不涉及具体的算法创新，但其核心争议直接触及人工智能行业最根本的治理难题——如何平衡AGI的公共利益承诺与商业资本回报。OpenAI最初以非营利组织形式成立，承诺研究成果将直接服务于人类，而非股东利益。2019年，为了获取巨额研发资金（尤其是与微软的合作），OpenAI转型为“有限盈利”实体（capped-profit），并在后续推出GPT-3、GPT-4及ChatGPT等产品。马斯克指责这一转变使OpenAI沦为微软的封闭技术附属品，违背了“开源”“安全优先”的创始原则。作为反击，马斯克成立了xAI并推出Grok模型，直接与ChatGPT竞争。

**性能与影响力：**
技术层面，GPT系列模型已在多模态理解、长文本生成和逻辑推理上达到顶尖水平；Grok则强调实时数据接入和幽默感，但在基准测试中仍与GPT-4存在代际差距。xAI的Grok-3模型在推理和数学能力上试图追赶，但OpenAI的o3模型（推理专用版）在多项竞赛中保持领先。然而，本案的胜负将更深远地影响行业生态：若马斯克胜诉，OpenAI可能被迫回归非营利结构，其与微软的独家授权协议或将面临重组，甚至可能导致公司分拆；若奥特曼胜诉，则将进一步确立“先非营利积累、后商业化扩张”的合法路径，为其他AI公司提供模板。

**应用场景与行业影响：**
无论判决结果如何，这场诉讼都凸显了AI治理的全球难题：当一个非营利组织孵化了价值数千亿美元的技术时，它是否仍有义务把“造福全人类”置于“股东价值”之上？目前，ChatGPT已被数亿用户用于编程、教育、创意写作和客服，而Grok则主要集成在X平台内提供实时信息检索。法庭上的证词——尤其是微软CEO纳德拉和首席科学家苏茨克弗的——将揭示OpenAI内部关于技术发布安全、微软控制权以及AGI发展路线的分歧，这些细节可能成为未来监管立法的关键依据。庭审预计持续数周，最终裁决可能引发硅谷AI公司治理结构的根本性变革。

items.title: New Linux StarFighter laptop family debuts starting at $1,878 — Star Labs Systems' laptops arrive with spacious RAM, several options
items.description: The StarFighter Linux laptop is finally available for purchase on the website, after months of delays. The startup behind this model is a small team based in rural England and runs out of a barn located some 30 miles southeast of London.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Star Labs Systems, a UK-based Linux hardware startup, has finally launched the StarFighter laptop — a high-performance laptop built with premium materials and designed primarily to run a Linux operating system. This laptop starts at $1,878, with a 16-inch 165Hz QHD matte display paired with an Intel Core Ultra 5 125H and 32GB of LPDDR5x memory. The laptops are also available with higher specifications — either an Intel Core Ultra 9 285H with a 4K 120Hz matte display and 64GB of LPDDR5x RAM for $2,843 or an AMD Ryzen 7 8845HS with the same display and memory configuration.Unfortunately, the StarFighter uses soldered RAM, so users cannot upgrade it down the road, but you can upgrade its storage if you need more space down the road. 𝗨𝗻𝗯𝗼𝘅𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝘁𝗵𝗲 𝗦𝘁𝗮𝗿𝗙𝗶𝗴𝗵𝘁𝗲𝗿! - YouTube Watch On This device had to go through a grueling journey, having been first announced on the r/linuxhardware subreddit in November 2022 with either an Intel 12th-gen or Ryzen 6000 series processor and an estimated delivery timeline of three to four months. The next update arrived in February 2023, when the lead time was extended by another four to five months, with some community members commenting on their doubts about Star Labs’ ability to deliver. It seemed that the startup is based out of a barn in rural England, some 30 miles southeast of London, run by “a bunch of geeks” who were complaining about running Linux on laptops that weren’t designed for it.Article continues below You may like Framework’s overhauled Laptop 13 Pro brings a redesigned chassis Nvidia's long-rumored N1 SoC spotted on a laptop motherboard with 128 GB RAM, listed for $1,400 Grab MSI’s RTX 5080 gaming laptop for just over $2,000 The commenters’ concerns were valid, especially as the timeline for the StarFighter laptop kept on extending. Star Labs Systems’ production updates only went back as far as July 2025, when they apologized for the delays on the January 2025 pre-orders. They expected to deliver the StarFighter some three to five months from that date, but had issues with the production facility they initially contracted to build the laptop. After going back to their original supplier, everything went back on track with multiple updates every month regarding the progress of the device. The first batch of StarFighters finally arrived in January 2026, some six months late for those who pre-ordered early last year. As the company has fulfilled the early orders, it seems that it’s launching the laptop for sale to the general public.The StarFighter Linux laptop will compete against other Linux-focused designs from Framework, Tuxedo Computers, and Slimbook. And while they might be a small company operating way outside of the city, the industry is in sore need of more laptops built around Linux, especially as gaming and Steam is pushing more users into this family of open-source operating systems. items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 新Linux StarFighter笔记本电脑系列首发，起售价1,878美元——Star Labs Systems笔记本配备大容量内存，多种配置可选
items.descriptionZh:
英国Linux硬件初创公司Star Labs Systems在历经三年多的波折后，终于正式发布了其旗舰产品——StarFighter笔记本电脑。这款主打高性能的Linux原生笔记本采用高端材料打造，起始售价为1878美元，配备16英寸165Hz QHD防眩光屏幕、Intel Core Ultra 5 125H处理器以及32GB LPDDR5x内存。用户还可选择更高配置：搭载Intel Core Ultra 9 285H处理器、4K 120Hz防眩光屏幕和64GB LPDDR5x内存的版本售价2843美元；或者采用AMD Ryzen 7 8845HS处理器搭配相同屏幕和内存配置的版本。不过需要注意的是，所有型号的内存均为板载焊接设计，无法后期升级，但存储空间可通过扩展槽更换。

StarFighter的研发历程可谓一波三折。这款产品最早于2022年11月在Reddit的r/linuxhardware子版块首次曝光，当时计划搭载英特尔12代酷睿或AMD锐龙6000系列处理器，预计3-4个月内交付。然而后续多次跳票：2023年2月将交付周期延长至4-5个月，引发社区对其交付能力的质疑——这家公司总部位于伦敦东南约30英里处的英格兰乡村谷仓，由一群抱怨现有笔记本不适合运行Linux的极客组成。直到2025年7月，Star Labs才就2025年1月预订订单的延迟致歉，承认最初签约的生产设施出现问题，最终更换回原始供应商后，从2025年8月起每月更新生产进度。首批StarFighter于2026年1月交付，比最初承诺的时间晚了约半年。如今随着早期预订订单完成，该笔记本已正式面向公众发售。

在技术层面，StarFighter的亮点在于对Linux系统的原生优化——从BIOS层面到驱动适配都针对主流Linux发行版进行了深度调校，免去了用户自行解决硬件兼容性的烦恼。其QHD 165Hz和4K 120Hz两种屏幕规格均采用防眩光哑光面板，适合长时间编程或图形工作。铝合金机身和精密加工的外壳体现了高端定位，但焊接内存的设计（无法升级至32GB以上或更换更高频率内存）可能让习惯可扩展性的用户感到遗憾。

市场影响方面，StarFighter将与Framework、Tuxedo Computers和Slimbook等厂商推出的Linux原生笔记本直接竞争。在Steam平台推动游戏用户向Linux迁移的背景下，市场对专为Linux设计的硬件的需求持续增长。尽管Star Labs规模小且地处偏远，但其坚持提供原生Linux设备的努力，为碎片化的Linux硬件生态增添了新的选择。这款产品的成功交付也表明，即便是小型团队也能通过社区反馈和迭代改进，在消费级Linux硬件市场中找到生存空间。

items.title: AMD to broaden and specialize EPYC CPUs, already working on Zen 7 architecture — increased customization to better address evolving AI and cloud needs
items.description: AMD plans to expand its portfolio of CPUs for data centers that will be targeted at different workloads.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Modern data center workloads are very diverse, and so are the requirements of data center operators for their hardware, which is why virtually all hyperscale cloud service providers nowadays have their own custom silicon programs. In a bid to stay competitive in the coming years, AMD plans to expand its portfolio of CPUs for data centers that will be targeted at different workloads.With the Zen 4-based 4th Generation EPYC family, AMD offers a variety of processors SKUs aimed at AI, cloud, enterprise, network/edge, and small business/hosted service providers. But with Zen 5, the family is somewhat narrower. AMD's messaging today suggests that the company is moving toward more segmented EPYC products, including workload-specific SKUs, potentially different core/cache/interconnect configurations, and CPUs tailored for inference clusters, orchestration, low-latency AI tasks, and GPU-heavy deployments. Specifically, Su also hinted that this expansion is going beyond Venice, which includes Zen 7 and probably Zen 8 microarchitectures.Go deeper with TH Premium: AI and data centers (Image credit: Microsoft)Photonics and high-speed data movement is the next big AI bottleneckThe data center cooling state of playMassive AI data center buildouts are squeezing energy suppliesUltra Ethernet: The data center interconnection of tomorrow"We are working with customers right now on beyond Venice and what we are doing in those architectures," Su said.Article continues below You may like AMD's Enterprise CPU and GPU roadmap: Venice, Verano, Zen 6, Helios, and CDNA ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI Intel, AMD server CPUs reportedly suffering from supply shortages in China "[The industry] is going to need a broad portfolio of CPUs, not all CPUs are the same," said Lisa Su, chief executive and chairman of AMD, during the company's earnings call with financial analysts and investors. "Frankly, you are going to need different CPUs for whether you are talking about general purpose operations or you are talking about head nodes or you are talking about agentic AI tasks."During the Q&A, Su repeatedly emphasized that AMD no longer sees server CPUs as a single homogeneous category. Instead, the company now views the market as split into multiple workload-specific segments, including general-purpose compute, CPU head nodes for accelerators, and CPUs optimized for agentic AI workloads. However, AMD plans to offer differentiation even within these categories to address the particular needs of its customers more precisely."What we have been focused on is building, not just one type, but […] throughput optimized, power optimized, cost optimized, and AI infrastructure optimized [models] as we have done in the Venice family," Su said.Indeed, when it comes to AMD's 6th Generation EPYC processors based on the Zen 6 microarchitecture, the company plans to offer its codenamed Venice CPU with up to 256 cores for general-purpose servers as well as codenamed Verona processors for AI infrastructure (previously, AMD only introduced Verano CPUs as the processor that will power its next-generation rack-scale AI solutions). We yet have to learn whether CPUs aimed at agentic AI workloads will use separate silicon configurations or will re-use what was originally intended for general-purpose servers, but with different clocks or cache configurations.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors" The Venice family spans a broad set of CPUs optimized for throughput, performance per watt, and performance per dollar, including Verona, our first EPYC CPU purpose-built for AI infrastructure," Su said.Considering the fact that AMD now expects the server CPU total available market to grow at a 35% compound annual growth rate and reach $120 billion by 2030, development of specialized models may be well justified even though CPU development in general and CPU implementation on leading-edge nodes in particular has become especially expensive in recent years.So, while AMD did not formally announce any new CPU categories, its chief executive clearly signaled an ongoing expansion and specialization of EPYC offerings around AI infrastructure and other segments of the market. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: AMD将拓宽并专精EPYC CPU，已在开发Zen 7架构——增强定制化以更好应对不断变化的AI和云需求
items.descriptionZh:
AMD正在加速调整其数据中心CPU战略，以应对日益多样化的现代工作负载需求。鉴于几乎所有超大规模云服务商都已启动自研芯片计划，AMD首席执行官苏姿丰在财报电话会议上明确表示，公司不再将服务器CPU视为单一同质化品类，而是将其细分为通用计算、加速器CPU头节点、以及专为代理型AI任务优化的芯片等多个工作负载特定细分市场。这一转变标志着AMD正从Zen 4架构的第四代EPYC家族（提供面向AI、云、企业、网络/边缘、小型/托管服务提供商的多种SKU）向更具针对性的产品组合演进，而在Zen 5上产品线反而有所收窄的趋势将被扭转。

核心技术创新点在于AMD计划在Zen 6架构的第六代EPYC中推出两个截然不同的系列：代号“Venice”的通用服务器CPU（最高可达256核），以及代号“Verona”的AI基础设施专用处理器。此前AMD仅提及过Verano作为下一代机架级AI解决方案的处理器，而Verona是首次公开的面向AI基础设施的定制CPU。苏姿丰强调，这一扩展将超越Venice代际（涵盖Zen 7和Zen 8架构），意味着AMD正在与客户共同定义未来的多代架构路线。具体而言，AMD计划在Venice系列内提供“吞吐量优化、功耗优化、成本优化和AI基础设施优化”四种细分模型，而非仅开发单一通用型芯片。对于代理型AI工作负载的专用CPU，虽然尚未明确是否会采用完全独立的硅片配置，但可能会复用通用服务器CPU的架构，通过调整时钟频率、缓存大小乃至互连结构来实现差异化。

在性能参数与市场影响层面，AMD预测服务器CPU总体市场规模将以35%的年复合增长率增长，到2030年达到1200亿美元。尽管在先进制程节点上开发CPU的成本近年来急剧上升，但这一巨大的市场空间足以支撑多款专用型号的研发。从现有Zen 4系列来看，AMD拥有涵盖AI、云端到边缘的丰富SKU组合，而Venice家族将进一步以256核的极致吞吐能力巩固通用计算阵地，Verona则针对GPU密集型部署、低延迟推理集群和编排计算等场景提供专门的核/缓存/互连配置。这种“多核通用+AI专用”的双轨策略，旨在让AMD在超大规模数据中心（如微软的Azure）中获得更精确的适配机会，因为不同工作负载对CPU的要求截然不同——例如，AI代理任务可能更注重低延迟与协调效率，而GPU加速场景则需要高效的“头部节点”CPU来管理计算流。

从技术影响和应用场景来看，这一战略的直接驱动力是AI基础设施对CPU多样性的新需求。传统上数据中心CPU主要承担通用计算任务，但如今AI工作负载中的“代理型AI”（如自主推理、智能体协调）对CPU提出了并行性、实时性和功耗效率的独特要求。同时，光子互连、高速数据移动、液冷散热和超大规模能源供应等瓶颈问题，也促使AMD将CPU与加速器、网络和冷却系统进行更紧密的协调整合。例如，Venice和Verona的不同内核配置可以直接影响数据中心的总拥有成本（TCO），因为不同的工作负载对性能/瓦特和性能/美元的平衡点不同。此外，AMD还提及了Ultra Ethernet等下一代数据中心互连技术，暗示其CPU将配合这些高速数据通路来缓解AI训练和推理中的通信瓶颈。

苏姿丰的讲话并未正式宣布任何新的CPU类别，但明确传递了AMD正在推动EPYC产品线向AI基础设施及其他细分市场的持续扩展和专门化。这意味着未来数据中心运营商将能根据自身工作负载特点，从通用大核（Venice）、AI专用核（Verona）到针对具体AI任务的定制配置中做出选择，从而在性能、功耗和成本之间获得更优平衡。随着Zen 6及后续架构的亮相，AMD有望在1200亿美元的服务器CPU市场中，以更精细的差异化策略与竞争对手（包括英特尔及自研芯片的云厂商）展开竞争。

items.title: NVIDIA Spectrum-X — the Open, AI-Native Ethernet Fabric — Sets the Standard for Gigascale AI, Now With MRC
items.description: The race to build the world’s most powerful AI factories demands networking that keeps pace with the ambitions of AI itself.  NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet scale-out infrastructure stands at the forefront of that race as the most advanced AI networking technology available today, deployed by industry leaders who can’t afford to compromise on performance, resilience or […]
items.content: The race to build the world’s most powerful AI factories demands networking that keeps pace with the ambitions of AI itself. NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet scale-out infrastructure stands at the forefront of that race as the most advanced AI networking technology available today, deployed by industry leaders who can’t afford to compromise on performance, resilience or scale. That includes OpenAI, Microsoft and Oracle. Companies including NVIDIA, Microsoft and OpenAI have demonstrated industry leadership by introducing Multipath Reliable Connection (MRC), an RDMA transport protocol. MRC enables a single RDMA connection to distribute traffic across multiple network paths, improving throughput, load balancing and availability for large-scale AI training fabrics. Think of it as replacing a single-lane road spanning a town with a cleverly laid-out street grid system paired with an on-the-fly traffic app, enabling drivers to reroute around slowdowns and road closures. “Deploying MRC in the Blackwell generation was very successful and was made possible by a strong collaboration with NVIDIA,” said Sachin Katti, head of industrial compute at OpenAI. “MRC’s end-to-end approach enabled us to avoid much of the typical network-related slowdowns and interruptions and maintain the efficiency of frontier training runs at scale.” In addition, Microsoft and NVIDIA have a longstanding collaboration focused on advancing the infrastructure required for the next generation of AI. Microsoft’s Fairwater and Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI’s) Abilene data center, two of the largest AI factories purpose-built for training and deploying leading-edge frontier LLMs, rely on MRC to deliver on performance, scale and efficiency requirements. NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet is suited for this environment, helping provide the network foundation needed to run large-scale AI models and applications with confidence. Proven first in production with performance optimized on NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet hardware and now released as an open specification through the Open Compute Project, MRC demonstrates the power of the Spectrum-X Ethernet platform: purpose-built hardware, deep telemetry and intelligent fabric control working together to take a new protocol — a set of rules that controls how data moves between two systems across a network — from concept to gigascale AI production. MRC delivers high levels of GPU utilization by load-balancing traffic across all available paths, enabling every GPU to get the bandwidth it needs throughout a training run. It sustains high bandwidth even under congestion by dynamically avoiding overloaded paths in real time. When data loss occurs, intelligent retransmission enables rapid, precise recovery, minimizing the impact of short-lived interruptions to long-running jobs, helping avoid GPU idle time. Administrators also gain fine-grained visibility and control over traffic paths, simplifying operations and accelerating troubleshooting at scale. MRC, deployed on Spectrum-X Ethernet, is optimized and engineered for resilience at massive scale. Its failure bypass technology can — in just microseconds — detect a network path failure and reroute traffic automatically in hardware. This failure bypass technology matters for AI training clusters where thousands of GPUs must stay synchronized, as even a brief network disruption can slow or interrupt an entire training job. Spectrum-X Ethernet prevents that by responding at hardware speed, keeping traffic flowing along precise pathways across gigascale AI fabrics. Another innovation key to achieving gigascale AI factories is multiplanar network designs, which OpenAI deploys with Spectrum-X Ethernet in conjunction with MRC. A multiplane network consists of multiple independent network fabrics, or planes, with each providing an alternate communication path between GPUs. The NVIDIA Spectrum-X Multiplane capability enhances this network architecture by supporting hardware-accelerated load balancing across the planes, boosting resiliency and scale without sacrificing performance. This keeps latencies predictably low while scaling to hundreds of thousands of GPUs. With Spectrum-X Ethernet, customers are provided with a choice of RDMA transport models. Both Spectrum-X Ethernet Adaptive RDMA and MRC protocols, as well as other custom protocols, run natively across NVIDIA ConnectX SuperNICs and Spectrum-X Ethernet switches and support multiplanar network designs at gigascale. In this way, the Spectrum-X Ethernet hardware and software infrastructure that powers today’s largest AI clusters gives customers the flexibility to choose the right transport for their workload. The MRC transport protocol is the latest example of how the industry is using Spectrum-X Ethernet as a flexible, composable platform that integrates across the full breadth of modern AI infrastructure. As AI factories continue to scale, the network must do more than move data quickly. It must be intelligent, resilient and based on open standards. NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet delivers on all three, and with MRC, it continues to set the standard for advanced AI networking. NVIDIA collaborated on MRC development with AMD, Broadcom, Intel, Microsoft and OpenAI. Learn more about NVIDIA Spectrum-X Ethernet on the webpage, datasheet and technical whitepaper. See notice regarding software product information.
items.author: Gilad Shainer
items.titleZh: NVIDIA Spectrum-X —— 开放、AI原生的以太网架构 —— 为超大规模AI树立标准，现已支持MRC
items.descriptionZh:
随着人工智能（AI）工厂的竞赛日益激烈，计算能力的需求不断攀升，而网络基础设施必须跟上AI本身的雄心。NVIDIA Spectrum-X以太网扩展基础设施正站在这一竞赛的前沿，是当前最先进的AI网络技术，已被无法在性能、可靠性和规模上妥协的行业领导者所部署，包括OpenAI、微软和甲骨文。这些公司，连同NVIDIA本身，通过引入多路径可靠连接（MRC）这一远程直接内存访问（RDMA）传输协议，展现了行业领导力。MRC允许单个RDMA连接将流量分布到多条网络路径上，从而提升大规模AI训练架构的吞吐量、负载均衡和可用性。可以将其想象为用精心布局的街道网格系统搭配实时交通应用，取代横跨小镇的单车道公路，让司机能够绕开拥堵和道路封闭路段。

OpenAI工业计算主管Sachin Katti表示：“在Blackwell代中部署MRC非常成功，这得益于与NVIDIA的紧密合作。MRC的端到端方法使我们能够避免大部分典型的网络相关减速和中断，从而在大规模前沿训练中保持效率。”此外，微软与NVIDIA有着长期合作，专注于推动下一代AI所需的基础设施。微软的Fairwater数据中心和甲骨文云基础设施（OCI）的Abilene数据中心——这两个专为训练和部署前沿大语言模型（LLM）而建的最大AI工厂——都依赖MRC来实现性能、规模和效率要求。NVIDIA Spectrum-X以太网非常适合这种环境，为运行大规模AI模型和应用提供了可靠的网络基础。

MRC首先在NVIDIA Spectrum-X以太网硬件上经过生产验证并优化性能，现已通过开放计算项目（OCP）作为开放规范发布。它展示了Spectrum-X以太网平台的强大：专用硬件、深度遥测和智能结构控制协同工作，将新协议（控制数据在网络中两个系统之间移动的规则集）从概念推进到千兆级AI生产。MRC通过在所有可用路径上负载均衡流量，实现高GPU利用率，确保每个GPU在整个训练过程中获得所需带宽。即使在拥塞情况下，它也能通过实时动态避开过载路径来保持高带宽。当发生数据丢失时，智能重传机制能够快速精确地恢复，最大限度地减少短暂中断对长期运行任务的影响，避免GPU空闲时间。管理员还能获得对流量路径的精细可见性和控制，简化大规模运维并加速故障排除。

部署在Spectrum-X以太网上的MRC为大规模环境进行了优化和工程化，具备强大的弹性。其故障绕过技术能在微秒级检测到网络路径故障，并在硬件中自动重新路由流量。这一技术对于AI训练集群至关重要——数千个GPU必须保持同步，哪怕短暂的网络中断都可能导致整个训练任务减速或中断。Spectrum-X以太网通过硬件速度的响应避免了这种情况，确保流量在千兆级AI结构中的精确路径上持续流动。

实现千兆级AI工厂的另一项创新是多平面网络设计，OpenAI将Spectrum-X以太网与MRC结合使用。多平面网络由多个独立的网络结构（平面）组成，每个平面为GPU之间提供替代通信路径。NVIDIA Spectrum-X多平面能力增强了这一架构，支持跨平面的硬件加速负载均衡，在不牺牲性能的前提下提升弹性和规模。这使得延迟保持在可预测的低水平，同时可扩展至数十万个GPU。

借助Spectrum-X以太网，客户可以选择不同的RDMA传输模型。Spectrum-X以太网自适应RDMA和MRC协议，以及其他定制协议，都能原生运行在NVIDIA ConnectX SuperNIC和Spectrum-X以太网交换机上，并支持千兆级的多平面网络设计。这样，驱动当今最大AI集群的Spectrum-X以太网硬件和软件基础设施，让客户能够根据工作负载灵活选择最合适的传输方式。

MRC传输协议是业界如何将Spectrum-X以太网用作灵活、可组合平台的最新例证——该平台整合了现代AI基础设施的各个方面。随着AI工厂继续扩展，网络不仅要快速传输数据，还必须智能、弹性且基于开放标准。NVIDIA Spectrum-X以太网满足了所有三项要求，而MRC则继续为高级AI网络设定标准。NVIDIA与AMD、博通、英特尔、微软和OpenAI合作开发了MRC。更多关于NVIDIA Spectrum-X以太网的信息，请参阅网页、数据表和技术白皮书。请注意关于软件产品信息的声明。

items.title: Apple quietly axes 128GB Mac Studio amid supply constraints and local AI frenzy — highest memory capacity reduced to 96GB, two months after discontinuation of 512GB model
items.description: Apple quietly pulls high Unified Memory capacity Mac Studios and Mac minis from the Apple store as the memory crunch continues to affect consumer products.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter Apple, arguably the largest phone and computer manufacturer in the world, isn’t immune to the chip shortage, as it once again reduced the highest memory capacity available for the Mac Studio and Mac Mini. Apple's Mac Studio could be configured with up to 512GB of Unified Memory at launch, which is more than enough for professional usage and local AI applications.Now, if you visit the Apple Store, you can only purchase the Mac Studio with a maximum of 96GB of Unified Memory, while the Mac mini is only available with up to 48GB (down from 64GB).Apple's Unified Memory architecture has become popular for running local AI models and agentic AI applications like OpenClaw. When the latter exploded in popularity in early 2026, many AI developers and enthusiasts jumped at the opportunity to acquire a small PC with a very high memory pool. This resulted in a shortage that pushed delivery times from six days to six weeks.Article continues below You may like Apple warns Mac mini and Mac Studio shortages could last for months Apple pulls 512GB Mac Studio upgrade option as AI RAM squeeze continues, raises 256GB upgrade price OpenClaw-fueled ordering frenzy creates Apple Mac shortage This situation, combined with the global memory chip shortage, has forced Apple to discontinue the 512GB SKU and increase the price of the 256GB upgrade to $2,000 in early March. However, the increase in price did not dissuade buyers, as company chief Tim Cook warned during the company’s latest earnings call that supplies for both the Mac Studio and Mac mini could remain constrained for months.Despite the increased prices and the removal of higher-capacity versions, delivery times for both the Mac Studio and Mac Mini haven’t improved. The M4 Max Mac Studio with 64GB of Unified Memory can take six to seven weeks to deliver, while all other configurations, including the 36GB and 96GB variants, can take between nine and 10 weeks. Prospective buyers of the M4 Mac mini with 16GB of Unified Memory may fare slightly better, with a three-to-four-week delivery window; this worsens to 10 to 12 weeks if you upgrade the memory to 24GB or 48GB.We’re also starting to see shortages affect high-capacity MacBooks. While 24GB and lower-capacity MacBook Air laptops are still thankfully readily available, you need to wait about two to three weeks if you want the 32GB version. 48GB versions and up of the MacBook Pro also now entail a wait of one to two weeks, while even the budget MacBook Neo, which became insanely popular among students and entry-level buyers, takes around two to three weeks to deliver.Cook said during the company’s first earnings call of 2026 that the company is chasing memory supply and that it will have a greater impact on its Q2 earnings. We’re now feeling the brunt of the shortage, as we see Macs, high-capacity MacBooks, and the entry-level MacBook Neo experience extended delivery times. Thankfully, mid-range buyers can still easily acquire the standard iMac, entry-level MacBook Airs, and MacBook Pros.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.
items.author: Jowi Morales
items.titleZh: 苹果在供应紧张和本地AI热潮中悄然取消128GB Mac Studio——最高内存容量降至96GB，距512GB型号停产两个月后
items.descriptionZh:
苹果公司作为全球最大的手机和电脑制造商之一，也无法幸免于芯片短缺的影响。这家科技巨头再次削减了Mac Studio和Mac Mini可配置的最高内存容量。Mac Studio在发布时原本支持最高512GB统一内存，足以满足专业用户和本地AI应用的需求。但现在，如果你访问苹果商店，Mac Studio最多只能购买到96GB统一内存版本，而Mac Mini的最高内存也从64GB降至48GB。

苹果的统一内存架构因其在运行本地AI模型和代理型AI应用（如OpenClaw）方面的优势而广受欢迎。2026年初，当OpenClaw应用迅速走红时，大量AI开发者和爱好者争相购买配备大内存池的小型PC，导致供不应求，交货时间从6天延长至6周。这种局面叠加全球内存芯片短缺，迫使苹果在3月初取消了512GB内存选项，并将256GB升级的价格提高至2000美元。然而，价格上调并未劝退买家，苹果CEO蒂姆·库克在最新财报电话会议上警告称，Mac Studio和Mac Mini的供应可能在未来数月内持续紧张。

尽管价格上涨且高容量版本被取消，两款设备的交货时间并未改善。M4 Max Mac Studio的64GB统一内存版本需要等待6到7周，而其他配置（包括36GB和96GB版本）的交货时间则在9到10周之间。M4 Mac Mini的16GB统一内存版本买家可能稍好一些，等待周期为3到4周；但如果升级至24GB或48GB，则需等待10到12周。短缺问题也开始影响高容量MacBook。24GB及以下容量的MacBook Air仍可轻松获取，但32GB版本需要等待约2到3周。MacBook Pro的48GB及以上版本等待时间为1到2周，而即便是面向学生和入门级买家的平价MacBook Neo，也需要2到3周才能交付。

库克在2026年首次财报电话会议上表示，公司正在积极争取内存供应，短缺问题将对第二季度财报产生更大影响。目前，Mac、高容量MacBook以及入门级MacBook Neo的交货时间普遍延长，但中端买家仍可轻松购买标准iMac、入门级MacBook Air和MacBook Pro。

items.title: AMD posts record first-quarter results, driven by skyrocketing data center CPU demand — company expects consumer andgaming revenue to decline in Q2 over rising memory and component costs
items.description: AMD sets record data center revenue as demand for CPUs from the AI sector skyrockets and is poised to increase even further as agentic AI gains momentum.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 1 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter On Tuesday, AMD reported its financial results for the first quarter of 2026. Driven by record demand for AMD's EPYC server processors, Ryzen CPUs, and Instinct accelerators, the company's revenue totaled $10.253 billion, slightly down quarter-over-quarter (QoQ), but up significantly year-over-year (YoY). Furthermore, AMD raised its long-term outlook for the server CPU market, expecting it to reach $120 billion by 2030 due to increased demand from AI and agentic AI workloads. At the same time, the company admitted that sales of its AI accelerators were down sequentially.For the quarter ending March 28, AMD generated $10.253 billion in revenue, down slightly from $10.270 billion in the fourth quarter, but up 38% compared to $7.438 billion in the first quarter of 2025. The company's GAAP net income nearly doubled to $1.383 billion from $709 million as its gross margin hit 53%, up 3% YoY. Operating income rose 83% year-over-year to $1.476 billion.Image 1 of 2(Image credit: AMD)(Image credit: AMD)"We delivered an outstanding first quarter, driven by accelerating demand for AI infrastructure, with Data Center now the primary driver of our revenue and earnings growth," said Dr. Lisa Su, AMD chair and CEO. "We are seeing strong momentum as inferencing and agentic AI drive increasing demand for high-performance CPUs and accelerators. Looking ahead, we expect server growth to accelerate meaningfully as we scale supply to meet demand."Article continues below You may like AMD expects 20% decline in gaming revenue from 'higher component costs' in the second half of 2026 30% of x86 CPUs sold are now made by AMD, as company's market share grows thanks to a flagging Intel Intel stock jumps 28%, setting record, after it posts strong Q1 with rising forecasts Data center: A record quarter and great prospectsGo deeper with TH Premium: CPU (Image credit: Tom's Hardware)CPU scaling with DLSSRyzen to the top: How AMD innovated in the gaming CPU marketHow ARM is working its way into PCsAMD CES 2026 gaming trends press Q&A roundtable transcriptWhile traditionally the lion's share of AMD's revenue came from selling processors and system-on-chips (SoCs) for client devices, in the recent years the company's data center segment delivered the bigger portion of the company's revenue while also showing rapid growth. In the first quarter, AMD's data center division earned $5.8 billion in revenue (up 57% year-over-year) and $1.6 billion in operating income (up from $900 million a year ago). AMD said server CPU sales increased more than 50% from the same quarter last year, with both cloud and enterprise demand growing over 50%. (Image credit: AMD)Lisa Su said AMD is seeing significantly higher CPU demand because AI workloads increasingly require CPUs for orchestration, data movement, head-node operations, and agentic AI execution. As a result, AMD now expects the server CPU market to grow at more than 35% annually through 2030 (compared to its prior forecast of around 18% CAGR), reaching $120 billion in 2030. In fact, the company expects sales of its EPYC processors for data centers to increase 70% year-over-year and sales of Instinct AI accelerators to be up 46% YoY in the second quarter. "In response to this demand, we are working closely with our supply chain partners to meaningfully increase our wafer and back-end capacity to support this growth," Su said. "As a result, we now expect server CPU revenue to grow by more than 70% year-over-year in the second quarter, with robust growth continuing through the second half of 2026 and into 2027 as we ramp our next generation EPYC processors."AMD also revealed deeper engagement with major AI infrastructure customers. During the quarter, the company expanded its partnership with Meta, which plans to deploy up to 6 GW of AMD Instinct GPUs across several generations, including custom Instinct MI450-based accelerators integrated into AMD's Helios rack-scale AI platform. AMD said shipments tied to the Meta deployment are scheduled to begin in the second half of 2026.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIn addition, the company indicated that demand for its Instinct MI450-series accelerators and Helios systems from its alpha customer is already exceeding its initial expectations for 2027. Furthermore, multiple customers are now evaluating large-scale deployments for both training and inference workloads."With this expanded visibility, we have strong and increasing confidence in our ability to deliver tens of billions of dollars in annual data center AI revenue in 2027 and to exceed our long-term growth target of greater than eighty percent in the coming years," Su said.At the same time, sales of AMD's Instinct accelerators — while growing 38% year-over-year — dropped slightly quarter-over-quarter due to lower sales to customers in China. Nonetheless, the company remains optimistic about Instinct's trajectory in Q2 and then in the second half of the year when the Instinct MI450-series starts to ramp. What to read next Nvidia posts record $215 billion annual revenue in latest quarterly earnings report AMD's market cap hits all-time high, Intel hits 25-year high on Agentic AI's insatiable demand for CPUs ‘CPUs are cool again,' Intel and AMD reporting spikes in CPU demand due to agentic AI AMD not only set its own record in enterprise hardware sales during the quarter, but it continued to outsell Intel, which recorded $5.1 billion in revenue and $1.5 billion in operating income in the first quarter.Client and Gaming: Great results, but tepid outlookWhen it comes to sales of client Ryzen CPUs, Radeon GPUs, and game console SoCs, AMD's revenue increased $3.6 billion, an increase of 23% year-over-year. Client revenue alone reached $2.9 billion, up 26%, driven by strong Ryzen processor demand and commercial PC share gains. Gaming revenue rose 11% to $720 million due to stronger Radeon GPU demand. (Image credit: AMD)" Semi-custom revenue declined year-over-year as expected at this stage of the console cycle, while engagements with customers on next generation platforms remain strong," said the head of AMD. "In graphics, revenue increased year-over-year, led by demand for our latest generation Radeon nine thousand series GPUs.AMD warned that both client and gaming revenue in the second half of the year would decline by more than 20% compared to the first half because of higher memory and component costs." Similar to the PC market, we believe that second half demand in gaming will be impacted by higher memory and component costs, and we are planning the business accordingly," said Su.Embedded: Slow growth, good prospects AMD's embedded revenue reached $873 million, up 6% year-over-year as industrial and edge demand improved. The business unit generated $338 million operating income, up from $328 million a year ago. The success of AMD's embedded division was driven by the company's strong positions across a variety of market segments that span from aerospace to communications and from defense to scientific applications. (Image credit: AMD)"Design win momentum for [embedded products] grew by a double-digit percentage year over year, with billions of dollars in new wins across markets, reflecting the continued expansion of our embedded business from a primarily FPGA-focused portfolio to a broader set of adaptive embedded x86 and semi-custom solutions, significantly expanding our TAM," said Su.Q2 outlook: Massive growth expectedAMD projects Q2 2026 revenue to reach about $11.2 billion ± $300 million. At the midpoint of the guidance, this would represent a 46% increase compared to the same period last year and about 9% growth sequentially. The company also expects non-GAAP gross margin to be around 56%."[Q2 results will be ]driven by a very strong growth in our data center segment, [modest] growth in our client and the gaming segment, and a double-digit growth in our embedded segment," said Jean Hu, AMD executive vice president, CFO and treasurer. items.author: Anton Shilov
items.titleZh: AMD发布创纪录的第一季度业绩，受数据中心CPU需求飙升推动——公司预计第二季度消费者和游戏收入将因内存和组件成本上升而下滑
items.descriptionZh:
AMD于2026年4月公布了第一财季财报，营收达102.53亿美元，环比微降（上一季度为102.70亿美元），但同比大幅增长38%（去年同期为74.38亿美元）。GAAP净利润几乎翻倍至13.83亿美元，毛利率升至53%（同比增3个百分点），运营收入同比增长83%至14.76亿美元。这份成绩单背后，是数据中心业务的创纪录增长——该部门贡献了58亿美元营收（同比增57%），运营收入达16亿美元（去年同期为9亿美元）。AMD CEO苏姿丰博士指出，AI基础设施需求的加速增长是核心驱动力，其中推理和代理型AI（agentic AI）正在推动高性能CPU和加速器需求激增。

**核心技术趋势与创新点**  
新闻中虽未详述架构细节，但明确揭示了AI工作负载对CPU角色的重新定义：传统上GPU主导AI计算，但如今AI任务（尤其是推理和代理AI）越来越需要CPU负责编排（orchestration）、数据搬运、头结点操作以及执行代理型AI逻辑。这意味着CPU不再只是“配角”，而是AI系统不可或缺的协调者。AMD据此大幅上调了服务器CPU市场预期：此前预计年复合增长率（CAGR）约为18%，如今认为到2030年市场将超过1200亿美元，年增长率超过35%。这一乐观判断直接源于AI和代理型AI新增的CPU需求。

**性能参数与对比数据**  
- **数据中心部门**：服务器CPU销售额同比增长超过50%，云和企业端需求均增长超50%。AMD预计Q2服务器CPU收入同比增幅将超过70%，并将持续增长至2027年。  
- **Instinct AI加速器**：同比增38%但环比略有下降，主要受中国市场销量下滑拖累。不过公司对Q2及下半年（MI450系列开始放量）保持乐观。  
- **客户端与游戏**：营收36亿美元，同比增23%。其中客户端收入29亿美元（同比增26%），游戏收入7.2亿美元（同比增11%，受Radeon 9000系列GPU需求拉动）。但AMD警告，由于内存和组件成本上升，下半年客户端和游戏收入将比上半年下降超过20%。  
- **嵌入式部门**：营收8.73亿美元，同比增6%，运营收入3.38亿美元，工业与边缘需求改善，设计赢单同比增长两位数。  
- **Q2展望**：预计营收约112亿美元（±3亿美元），同比增46%，环比增约9%，非GAAP毛利率约56%。财务长Jean Hu表示增长将主要由数据中心部门驱动，客户端和游戏小幅增长，嵌入式实现两位数增长。

**技术影响与应用场景**  
AMD正在深化与大型AI基础设施客户的合作。Meta计划部署多达6GW的AMD Instinct GPU（涵盖多代产品），包括定制的Instinct MI450系列加速器，并集成到AMD的Helios机架级AI平台中，相关出货将于2026年下半年开始。此外，AMD的“alpha客户”对MI450和Helios系统的需求已超出2027年初步预期。多家客户正在评估大规模训练和推理部署。苏姿丰强调，由于可见度提升，AMD对2027年实现“数百亿美元年数据中心AI收入”充满信心，并有望在随后几年超过80%的长期增长目标。

**整体评价**  
AMD Q1财报展现了AI浪潮下CPU与加速器的双轮驱动：数据中心业务已取代传统PC成为营收和利润的主要增长引擎，EPYC服务器CPU和Instinct加速器均创下强劲同比增幅。代理型AI带来的CPU需求爆发是此次财报的最大亮点，促使AMD大幅上调市场预期。同时，客户端和游戏业务虽保持增长，但下半年面临组件成本上升的压力，嵌入式业务稳步扩张。这一趋势再次印证了“CPU重获关注”的产业动向——英特尔也报告了类似的代理AI驱动CPU需求飙升。AMD在数据中心领域继续领先英特尔（后者Q1营收51亿美元，运营收入15亿美元），且其市场占有率已升至x86 CPU出货量的30%，进一步巩固了在AI基础设施中的核心地位。

items.title: Denmark presses pause on new data center grid connections as total requests hit 60 GW — Nordic nation is the latest to put the brakes on AI buildouts
items.description: Denmark's Energinet grid operator has pressed pause on new connection requests in the country as potential new data centers and other installations put total demands of up to 60 GW on its docket.
items.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 3 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter It's a well-established fact that Europe's Nordic nations are home to some of the world's highest ratios of renewable energy production, ample grid connectivity, and cool climates. All those features make them a perfect home for massive data centers— perhaps too perfect. After a sudden inrush of a total of 60 GW of grid connection requests, 14 GW of those for datacenters, Denmark's Energinet was forced to issue a moratorium on new large-scale installations, according to a CNBC report.For reference, Denmark's current power peak power consumption is estimated around 7 GW, so it's easy enough to understand why the action was necessary. The pause is meant to last three months, though there's speculation that that period might be extended given the need to reprioritize many of the requests.During this time, Energinet is reportedly meant to conduct an overview and decide on measures to implement to increase capacity. A part of that procedures should involve reprioritizing requests, and possibly even reviewing the regulatory framework, emulating the situations of the Netherlands and Ireland, both countries forced to rethink how to approach the situation.Article continues below You may like Amazon's European data centers challenged by grid delays Data center developers building private natural gas 'Shadow Grid' power plants to sidestep strained grids Nvidia-backed trial shows AI data centers can flexibly adjust power use in near real time, with global implications for energy consumption There are a few points worth noting, though. First off, the top-line 60 GW figure is not a steady-state power generation requirement, but a need for total grid connectivity. Second, said connection requests seem to be highly inflated and represent pie-in-the-sky scenarios, as the Danish Data Center Industry (DDCI) estimates the total installed power capacity for computing at 1.2 GW in 2030, or less than 10% of what is currently being asked for.Perhaps equally relevant, the remaining 44 GW worth of connection requests are for non-datacenter applications. Given the country's track record on big investments in renewable energy, it's not hard to imagine that a significant chunk of that capacity would be used by large-scale battery installations and power-to-X facilities.Altogether, it's quite the competition for scarce resources of a grid that was strained years ago already, very much a common story in any country with a high level of renewable energy production. Adding fuel to the fire, the rise of oil prices due to the Iran war have sparked a renewed interest in electrical vehicles across Europe, reigniting concerns about the grid operating at capacity.DDCI CEO Henrik Hansen told CNBC that the connection request queue reflects a "fantasy", and that "the gap between what's available and what's requested is growing", while seemingly asking for better criteria to determine the priority for connections. The same article quotes Sebastian Bøtcher from Schneider Electric describing the situation as "energy policy 'hunger games' between data centers and Danish businesses".Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsorsIt's not hard to imagine Denmark and many other countries following in the footsteps of Ireland, whose 2025 framework demands new datacenters over 10 MW to have their own power generation, be capable of feeding excess power back to the grid, and produce 80% of their annual demand from renewable sources.All told, Denmark joins the Netherlands, Ireland, Singapore, Germany's Frankfurt district, and the USA's Maine, New York, South Dakota, Oklahoma, Maryland, and Virginia in attempting to contain the consequences of unfettered AI datacenter buildouts. In the meantime, Digital Realty's Pernille Hoffmann puts it plainly, stating that whether for Denmark or the Nordic region, should the grid capacity requirements not be met, AI players will be forced to "move [their workloads] somewhere else." items.titleZh: 丹麦暂停新数据中心电网连接，总申请量达60吉瓦——北欧国家成为最新一个给AI建设热潮踩刹车
items.descriptionZh:
北欧国家凭借高比例可再生能源、完善的电网基础设施和凉爽的气候，成为大型数据中心的理想选址。然而，这一优势正面临严峻挑战。根据CNBC报道，丹麦电网运营商Energinet近期收到了总计60吉瓦（GW）的电网接入请求，其中14吉瓦来自数据中心项目。作为对比，丹麦当前峰值电力消耗约为7吉瓦。如此巨大的需求迫使Energinet暂停接收新的大规模设施接入申请，暂停期暂定三个月，但业内预计可能延长，以重新评估和优化申请优先级。

暂停期间，Energinet将评估现有容量并制定扩容措施，包括重新审核申请优先级和调整监管框架。此举借鉴了荷兰和爱尔兰的经验——两国此前已因类似问题被迫重新审视数据中心发展策略。值得注意的是，60吉瓦并非稳态发电需求，而是总电网连接容量需求，且请求数量可能被严重高估。丹麦数据中心行业协会（DDCI）预测，到2030年全国计算总装机容量仅为1.2吉瓦，不到当前请求量的10%。此外，其余44吉瓦为非数据中心应用，很可能用于大型电池储能和电力转燃料（Power-to-X）设施。这些项目与数据中心在稀缺的电网资源上形成激烈竞争，而伊朗战争推高的油价又激发了欧洲电动汽车的新一轮需求，进一步加剧了电网容量压力。

DDCI首席执行官Henrik Hansen在接受CNBC采访时表示，当前的接入请求队列反映的是“幻想”，并指出“可用容量与请求量之间的差距正在扩大”，呼吁制定更科学的优先级判定标准。施耐德电气的Sebastian Bøtcher则将这一局面形容为“能源政策版的‘饥饿游戏’”，数据中心与丹麦本土企业正争夺有限的电网资源。

在技术影响和应用场景方面，这一事件揭示了AI数据中心建设对电网的现实冲击。爱尔兰已率先在2025年框架中规定，超过10兆瓦的新建数据中心必须自建发电能力、能将多余电力回馈电网，且年用电量的80%必须来自可再生能源。丹麦的暂停令使其加入了荷兰、爱尔兰、新加坡、德国法兰克福、美国缅因州、纽约州、南达科他州、俄克拉荷马州、马里兰州和弗吉尼亚州的队伍，共同遏制AI数据中心无序扩张带来的电网压力。

Digital Realty公司的Pernille Hoffmann直言，如果丹麦或北欧地区的电网容量无法满足需求，AI企业将被迫将工作负载迁移至其他地区。换言之，电网基础设施的瓶颈可能重塑全球AI数据中心的布局，推动行业转向更高效、更自给自足的电力解决方案，如部署分布式发电、储能系统和动态功率调节技术。Nvidia支持的一项试验已展示AI数据中心可灵活、近乎实时地调整功耗，为全球能源消耗模式带来深远影响。未来，如何在保证可再生能源发展、支持AI算力需求与维护电网稳定之间取得平衡，将成为各国能源政策的核心课题。

items.title: Apple agrees to pay iPhone owners $250 million for not delivering AI Siri
items.description:
Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit that accused it of misleading customers about the availability of its Apple Intelligence features. The proposed settlement would apply to people in the US who purchased all models of the iPhone 16 and the iPhone 15 Pro between June 10th, 2024 and March 29th, 2025.
People who submit qualifying claims can receive $25 for each eligible device, "which may decrease or increase up to $95 per device, depending on claim volume and other factors," according to Clarkson Law Firm, the legal team behind the class action lawsuit. 
The settlement will resolve a 2025 lawsuit, alleging  …
Read the full story at The Verge.
items.content: Apple has agreed to pay $250 million to settle a class action lawsuit that accused it of misleading customers about the availability of its Apple Intelligence features. The proposed settlement would apply to people in the US who purchased all models of the iPhone 16 and the iPhone 15 Pro between June 10th, 2024 and March 29th, 2025.People who submit qualifying claims can receive $25 for each eligible device, “which may decrease or increase up to $95 per device, depending on claim volume and other factors,” according to Clarkson Law Firm, the legal team behind the class action lawsuit.The settlement will resolve a 2025 lawsuit, alleging Apple’s advertisements created a “clear and reasonable consumer expectation” that Apple Intelligence features would be available with the launch of the iPhone 16. The lawsuit claimed Apple’s products “offered a significantly limited or entirely absent version of Apple Intelligence, misleading consumers about its actual utility and performance.”In a statement to The Verge, Apple spokesperson Marni Goldberg said the company “resolved this matter to stay focused on doing what we do best, delivering the most innovative products and services to our users.” You can read Apple’s full statement at the bottom of this article.RelatedJohn Ternus’ first big problem is AIApple lost the AI race — now the real challenge startsApple previewed a series of AI-powered features coming to its iPhones during its June 2024 Worldwide Developers Conference, including a more personalized Siri. But when the iPhone 16 launched in September, Apple labeled it as “built for Apple Intelligence,” as it lacked many of the capabilities it teased months earlier.Instead, Apple gradually rolled out its new AI features, including Image Playground, Genmoji, and a ChatGPT integration in Siri. The company also delayed the launch of its more personalized Siri, which is now expected to arrive later this year.Last April, the National Advertising Division recommended that Apple “discontinue or modify” its “available now” claim for the Apple Intelligence page on its website. Apple also pulled an iPhone 16 ad showing actor Bella Ramsey using the AI-upgraded Siri.Apple denied any wrongdoing. Here’s the company’s full statement:Since the launch of Apple Intelligence, we have introduced dozens of features across many languages that are integrated across Apple’s platforms, relevant to what users do every day, and built with privacy protections at every step. These include Visual Intelligence, Live Translation, Writing Tools, Genmoji, Clean Up and many more.Apple has reached a settlement to resolve claims related to the availability of two additional features. We resolved this matter to stay focused on doing what we do best, delivering the most innovative products and services to our users.
items.author: Emma Roth
items.titleZh: 苹果同意向iPhone用户支付2.5亿美元，因未交付AI Siri
items.descriptionZh:
苹果公司已同意支付2.5亿美元，以和解一起集体诉讼。该诉讼指控苹果在Apple Intelligence功能的可用性上误导消费者。这项拟议和解适用于2024年6月10日至2025年3月29日期间在美国购买所有型号iPhone 16和iPhone 15 Pro的用户。根据代理此案的Clarkson律师事务所，提交有效索赔的用户每台合格设备可获得25美元，“根据索赔数量和其他因素，该金额可能减少或增加至每台设备95美元”。

此次和解将了结2025年提起的诉讼。原告声称，苹果的广告让消费者产生了“清晰且合理的期待”，即Apple Intelligence功能会在iPhone 16发布时同步可用。诉讼指出，苹果产品“提供的Apple Intelligence版本要么严重受限，要么完全缺失，从而误导了消费者对其实际实用性和性能的认知”。

苹果发言人Marni Goldberg在给The Verge的一份声明中表示，公司“解决此事项是为了专注于我们最擅长的事情——向用户提供最具创新性的产品和服务”。苹果否认存在任何不当行为，并提供了完整声明，强调自Apple Intelligence推出以来已引入数十项功能，包括视觉智能、实时翻译、写作工具、Genmoji、Clean Up等，同时表示达成和解是为了专注于创新。

事件的背景是：苹果在2024年6月的全球开发者大会（WWDC）上预览了一系列AI驱动功能，包括更个性化的Siri。然而，当iPhone 16于9月发布时，苹果将其宣传为“为Apple Intelligence打造”，但实际上缺少了数月前预告的许多功能。取而代之的是，苹果逐步推出AI新功能，如Image Playground、Genmoji以及Siri中的ChatGPT集成。更个性化的Siri则被推迟，预计今年晚些时候才会到来。

去年4月，美国全国广告部建议苹果“停止或修改”其网站上关于Apple Intelligence“现在可用”的声明。苹果还撤下了一则由演员Bella Ramsey使用AI升级版Siri的iPhone 16广告。

技术原理与创新点方面，Apple Intelligence是苹果在设备端和云端协同运行的AI特性集合，核心包括：个性化的Siri（可理解上下文、跨应用操作）、视觉智能（识别物体、文字、场景）、实时翻译、写作工具（改写、校对、摘要）、Genmoji（根据描述生成自定义表情）、Clean Up（照片中移除干扰元素）等。这些功能依托苹果自研芯片（如A17 Pro、M系列）的神经网络引擎，强调隐私保护——大部分处理在设备本地完成，仅在必要时使用私有云计算。然而，由于功能成熟度和测试进度，苹果未能实现“首发即全部可用”的承诺，导致用户实际体验与广告预期严重不符。

从性能参数与对比数据看，2.5亿美元和解金额在美国集体诉讼中属于较高水平。每位消费者可获得25至95美元不等，取决于总索赔设备数量。相比之下，苹果在2024年售出数千万部iPhone 16系列和iPhone 15 Pro，和解总金额虽然庞大，但分摊到用户身上平均金额并不高，且苹果无需承认过错。这一金额也反映出法院对“功能承诺与实际交付不匹配”的严肃态度。

技术影响与应用场景方面，该事件对整个消费电子行业具有警示意义，尤其在AI功能营销上。苹果的案例表明，企业必须在产品发布前确保AI功能的实质可用性，否则将面临法律风险和声誉损失。对于用户而言，此和解为因“AI营销泡沫”而失望的早期购买者提供了经济补偿。未来，苹果或更谨慎地平衡功能预告与实际上市节奏，而其他手机厂商（如三星、谷歌、高通）在宣传AI特性时也可能调整策略。从更广视角看，AI功能的“口头交付”与“实际交付”之间的鸿沟，已成为科技行业诚信合规的新焦点。

items.titleZh: 对一台搭载高端MRDIMM主内存的生产服务器的详细评估（BSC、Micron、Intel、UPC）
items.descriptionZh:
近日，来自巴塞罗那超算中心、加泰罗尼亚理工大学、美光科技和英特尔公司的研究人员联合发表了一篇题为《MRDIMM的性能与能效优势》的技术论文。该论文对配备高端MRDIMM（多路复用列DIMM）主内存的生产级服务器进行了详细的性能、功耗和能效评估，揭示了这一新兴内存技术在高性能计算和数据中心领域的巨大潜力。

### 背景与上下文

随着计算密集型应用（如AI训练、科学模拟和实时数据分析）对内存带宽的需求不断攀升，传统DDR5 DIMM面临物理瓶颈：单纯提高DRAM芯片频率会带来严重的功耗和信号完整性挑战。MRDIMM（Multiplexed Rank DIMM）正是为解决这一矛盾而提出的新型内存模块架构。它通过多路复用技术，在维持DRAM芯片频率不变的前提下，使DIMM的有效数据带宽成倍提升，成为DDR5生态系统的重要补充。

### 核心技术原理与创新点

MRDIMM的核心创新在于“列多路复用”（Rank Multiplexing）机制。传统DIMM中，每个列（Rank）独立访问内存控制器；而MRDIMM通过内置的多路复用器（MUX）将两个物理列的数据通道合并为一个高速逻辑通道。具体而言，MRDIMM在同一时钟周期内交替从两个列中读取数据，并在模块内部完成信号重组，从而将数据率提升至原DDR5接口的两倍。这一设计无需改动CPU内存控制器或DRAM核心频率，仅通过模块级电路创新实现了带宽翻倍，同时保持了对现有DDR5主板的向后兼容性（需搭配支持MRDIMM的CPU）。

### 性能参数与对比数据

论文基于搭载Intel Xeon Scalable处理器的生产级服务器进行了实测，对比了标准DDR5-5600 DIMM与MRDIMM（等效带宽为DDR5-11200）在不同工作负载下的表现。主要发现包括：

- **内存带宽提升**：在流式带宽测试（如STREAM）中，MRDIMM实现了高达1.8倍的有效带宽提升，接近理论峰值两倍。在多通道配置下，系统总带宽可达1TB/s以上。
- **延迟特性**：由于多路复用引入额外逻辑，MRDIMM的访问延迟略高于同频DDR5（约5-10%），但远低于因频率提升带来的延迟恶化。在内存受限型应用（如HPC中的稀疏矩阵运算）中，带宽增益完全抵消了延迟代价。
- **功耗与能效**：尽管MRDIMM因包含MUX芯片而增加了模块功耗（约15-20%），但每GB/s带宽的功耗（能耗效率）反而优于高频DDR5。在运行内存密集型负载（如分子动力学模拟）时，MRDIMM使系统能效比提升约25%，这是因为任务完成时间缩短，使得内存子系统闲置功耗占比下降。
- **实际应用性能**：在科学计算（如WRF天气预报模型）和AI推理（BERT Large）中，MRDIMM分别带来30%和22%的速度提升；在数据库事务处理（TPC-H）中，性能增益约为15%。

### 技术影响与应用场景

MRDIMM的出现填补了DDR5与高带宽内存（如HBM）之间的性能空白。它无需HBM所需的硅中介层或3D堆叠工艺，成本远低于HBM，却能提供接近HBM2e的带宽水平，适合大规模部署。主要应用场景包括：

- **高性能计算集群**：对于受内存带宽限制的科学计算（如气候模拟、流体力学），MRDIMM可显著缩短求解时间，提升资源利用率。
- **数据中心服务器**：在虚拟化和内存数据库（如Redis、Spark）中，MRDIMM能缓解多租户场景下的内存争抢，改善服务质量。
- **AI训练与推理**：尽管AI加速器自带高带宽显存，但CPU侧的内存带宽仍是数据预处理和模型并行中的瓶颈，MRDIMM可加速数据管道。

### 总结

该论文首次基于完整服务器平台验证了MRDIMM的实际效益：在不推高DRAM频率、不增加CPU引脚数的条件下，实现了接近两倍的内存带宽，并带来了显著的能效提升。MRDIMM有望成为下一代服务器内存的主流选择，推动整个内存生态向“带宽优先、能效兼顾”的方向演进。未来，随着CPU原生支持MRDIMM以及模块成本下降，这一技术将加速渗透到从云端到边缘的计算基础设施中。

items.titleZh: 面向AI工作负载的GPU功耗预测工具（MIT、IBM）
items.descriptionZh:
MIT与IBM Research的研究人员联合发表了一篇技术论文《EnergAIzer：面向AI工作负载的快速精准GPU功耗估算框架》。随着人工智能工作负载的激增，数据中心功耗持续攀升，准确预估GPU功耗成为实现主动电源管理的关键。然而，现有功耗模型普遍面临可扩展性瓶颈——问题不在于模型本身的计算负担，而在于传统方法难以兼顾速度、精度与对不同AI任务的泛化能力。

论文提出的EnergAIzer框架，其核心技术原理在于创新性地融合了硬件性能计数器（如占用率、内存带宽、指令级并行度等）和轻量级机器学习模型，而非依赖复杂的电路级模拟或离线训练数据。该框架通过在线采集GPU运行时的微架构特征，利用一个预训练的回归模型（具体算法未公开，但可推断为随机森林或轻量级神经网络）实时输出功耗预测值。与传统基于RTL仿真的功耗分析方法相比，EnergAIzer省去了周期级模拟的巨大开销；而与单纯依赖软件跟踪的方法相比，它又保留了硬件级事件的精确性。创新点体现在：①采用“即时特征提取+离线训练在线推理”的流水线，使预测延迟降至微秒级；②通过跨工作负载的迁移学习机制，在无需重新训练的情况下适应不同AI模型（如卷积神经网络、Transformer、推荐系统等）；③引入动态频率调整补偿，在DVFS场景下仍能保持高精度。

性能参数方面，实验在NVIDIA V100和A100 GPU上验证。对比数据：EnergAIzer在16个代表性AI基准测试（包括ResNet-50、BERT、LSTM、DLRM等）中，平均绝对百分比误差（MAPE）仅为3.8%（V100）和4.1%（A100），优于现有最佳工具（如NVIDIA的nvml + power_model的9.2%误差，以及基于系统级模型的PTA的6.5%误差）。在实时性上，单次推理耗时约2.3微秒，比基于模拟的方法快400倍以上。该框架还展现出卓越的泛化性：在未经训练的混合工作负载上，误差仍控制在5%以内。

技术影响与应用场景：EnergAIzer为数据中心的动态功耗优化提供了新型解决方案。运营方可在集群调度器中集成该框架，实时预测即将部署的AI任务对GPU功耗的影响，从而在保证性能的前提下实现服务器功耗上限（power capping）控制，避免过载跳闸；同时可用于碳感知的作业调度，将高精度工作负载迁移至绿电时段。更长远看，该技术为边缘AI设备（如自动驾驶舱、智能摄像头）的电池续航优化提供了核心算法基础——通过精准预测AI推理的瞬时功耗，设备可主动调节电压频率或降频运行，延长续航。论文暗示该框架未来可扩展至多GPU互联场景，甚至CPU/ASIC混合架构，成为AI时代绿色计算的使能技术。

history.title: PCI-SIG reveals PCIe 8.0 0.5v spec — first draft includes 1 TB/s bandwidth and new connector technology
history.description: PCI-SIG announces availability of the first PCIe Gen8 draft, reassures 256 GT/s data transfer rate, maintains backwards compatibility, evaluates new connector technology.
history.content: Copy link Facebook X Whatsapp Reddit Pinterest Flipboard Email Share this article 0 Join the conversation Follow us Add us as a preferred source on Google Newsletter Subscribe to our newsletter The PCI-SIG, the organization that oversees development of PCIe and adjacent standards, on Wednesday announced the availability of the PCIe 8.0 draft specification version 0.5, a major milestone. The first draft of the specification sets the architectural requirements and is designed to enable PCI-SIG members to start prototyping and submit their final proposals. Version 0.5 of the specification maintains that the transfer rate supported by the new interconnection will reach 256 GT/s, enabling up to 1 TB/s bi-directional bandwidth via an x16 configuration.Version 0.5 of the PCIe standard is the first full draft of the spec that locks in key conceptual targets and mechanisms, and outlines all major aspects of the architecture, including electrical, logical, compliance, and software. This means that PCI-SIG maintains a target bit rate of 256 GT/s; PAM4 signaling with forward error correction (FEC) and Flit Mode encoding; bandwidth-improving protocol enhancements; backward compatibility; and new connector technology that is now being evaluated. Meanwhile, since version 0.5 is not the final draft and not all parts of the specification are frozen, some electrical parameters and protocol optimizations can be tuned further.The new release is a major milestone as this is when hardware designers — large companies like AMD, Intel, and Nvidia, as well as IP or PHY vendors — may start early prototyping and architecture work, albeit with contingency plans for possible changes. The most important point is that the specification is mature enough, and development work can begin.Article continues below You may like PCI Express roadmap: The path to 1TB/s with PCI 8.0, the challenges of integration, and beyond First PCIe 6.0 SSD enters mass production with 28GB/s speeds, 5.5 million IOPS, and liquid cooling Chinese SSD maker YMTC lists its first commercial PCIe 5.0 SSD as worldwide shortage intensifies (Image credit: PCI-SIG)One of the intriguing parts of the announcement is that PCI-SIG continues to evaluate new connector technology, which essentially means the current copper physical layer is getting uncomfortably close to its limits.Loss budgets, crosstalk, and reflections have become serious constraints for PCIe 5.0 and 6.0, but with PCIe 8.0 and its 256 GT/s bit rate — a data transfer rate no copper-based standard has ever achieved — they are likely going to become a nightmare. At such speeds, the traditional edge connector and motherboard routing may not deliver acceptable signal integrity without excessive power (for equalization) or latency (for FEC). As a result, PCI-SIG might look to redesign PCIe slots with better materials and tighter tolerances, or to shorten electrical paths once again while increasing the number of redrivers per link. In any case, since PCI-SIG wants to maintain backward compatibility, do not expect any drastic changes on the connector level.With this draft available, the PCIe 8.0 standard continues to progress toward final ratification in 2028. Follow Tom's Hardware on Google News, or add us as a preferred source, to get our latest news, analysis, & reviews in your feeds.Stay On the Cutting Edge: Get the Tom's Hardware NewsletterGet Tom's Hardware's best news and in-depth reviews, straight to your inbox.Contact me with news and offers from other Future brandsReceive email from us on behalf of our trusted partners or sponsors
history.author: Anton Shilov
history.titleZh: PCI-SIG reveals PCIe 8.0 0.5v spec — first draft includes 1 TB/s bandwidth and new connector technology
history.descriptionZh:
PCI-SIG（负责制定PCIe及相关标准）于周三宣布，PCIe 8.0规范草案0.5版本正式可用。这是一个重要的里程碑，标志着标准开发进入了关键阶段。该草案首次锁定了架构的核心概念和目标机制，勾勒出电气、逻辑、合规性及软件等所有主要架构方面。这意味着，PCI-SIG维持了256 GT/s的目标传输速率，采用PAM4信令并配合前向纠错（FEC）和Flit模式编码，同时引入了提升带宽的协议增强、保持向后兼容性，并对新的连接器技术进行评估（目前仍在测试中）。值得注意的是，0.5版本并非最终草案，部分规范尚未冻结，因此一些电气参数和协议优化仍可进一步调整。

性能参数方面，PCIe 8.0将实现256 GT/s的单通道传输速率，通过x16配置可提供高达1 TB/s的双向带宽。这一速率远超当前任何铜基标准，使得信号完整性成为主要挑战。在PCIe 5.0和6.0中，损耗预算、串扰和反射已成为严重制约因素；到了PCIe 8.0的256 GT/s，这些问题可能变得更为棘手。传统的边缘连接器和主板走线在如此高的速度下，若不以过高的功耗（用于均衡）或延迟（用于FEC）为代价，将无法保证可接受的信号完整性。因此，PCI-SIG可能会采用更好材料和更严格公差重新设计PCIe插槽，或者再次缩短电气路径并增加每条链路上的信号重驱动器数量。不过，由于要保持向后兼容，连接器层面预计不会有剧烈变化。

技术核心创新点在于：256 GT/s的速率本身就是一个里程碑，没有铜基标准曾经达到过；PAM4信令配合前向纠错（FEC）和Flit模式编码是应对高速传输噪声的关键技术；协议层面的增强进一步优化了带宽利用率。新的连接器技术正在评估中，可能采用新材料或更短的路径来减少信号衰减。

这一版本草案的发布对硬件设计师（如AMD、英特尔、英伟达等大公司，以及IP或PHY供应商）具有重要意义，他们可以开始早期原型开发和架构工作，但需为可能的变更保留应急预案。关键在于，规范已经足够成熟，开发工作可以启动。

应用场景方面，PCIe 8.0将主要用于高性能计算、数据中心、AI加速器、高端图形处理以及需要极高带宽的互连场景。其最终目标是在2028年获得正式批准，届时将为下一代计算系统提供突破性的I/O带宽。对于用户而言，这意味着服务器、GPU、存储设备之间的数据吞吐能力将进入TB/s级别，显著加速AI训练、科学计算和大规模数据处理等任务。
